description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the python:3.6 Docker image with a global before_script that echoes $HOME, sets VERSION=`cat angreal/VERSION`, installs virtualenv, creates a virtualenv at ~/.venv/angreal, activates it, and runs pip install .[dev]. Define stages test, release, post_release, and pages. In the test stage add functional-test (image python:3.6, runs `doit functional_tests`), install-test (runs `pip install .`), and unit-test (runs `export GITLAB_CI=1` then `doit tests`, captures coverage with regex /TOTAL.+ ([0-9]{1,3}%)/); all three run on all branches except tags and do not allow failure. In the release stage add cleanup_release (runs `rm -vf ~/.pypirc`, only on tags, always), release (before_script checks that CI_BUILD_TAG equals VERSION, then runs `pip install twine`, `pip install .[dev]`, writes ~/.pypirc with PYPI_USER and PYPI_PASSWORD, builds with `python setup.py check sdist bdist_wheel`, uploads with `twine upload --repository pypi dist/*`, then removes .pypirc; manual, only tags), and release-test (allow_failure true, runs `pip install twine`, writes a testpypi ~/.pypirc, builds and uploads with `twine upload --repository testpypi dist/*`, installs the package from testpypi, and cleans .pypirc; only tags). In the post_release stage add init_data_science_project, init_gitlab_python, init_meeting_minutes, init_presentation, and init_template jobs (each allow_failure true, before_script `echo ""`, image python:3, only tags, script `pip install angreal` then `angreal init <name> --no-input` with extra flags for gitlab_python). Finally, in the pages stage add trigger_docs (only on master, before_script `echo "No need to setup"`, script `curl --request POST --form "token=$GITLAB_TRIGGER_TOKEN" --form ref=master https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/$APP_PROJECT_ID/trigger/pipeline`, variable APP_PROJECT_ID set to 12432876).
